commit.message:
chore: bump version to 1.1.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: improve TUI tutorial UX with search, back-nav, and rich styling

- Add /search filtering in tutorial list (substring match on id/title)
- Add back-navigation stack so Esc returns to the originating screen
- Add t keybinding on InstanceDetail screen for tutorial access
- Improve tutorial view rendering: cyan headings, yellow commands,
  green keybindings, cyan bullets, scroll percentage indicator
- Show empty state when no tutorials match search
- Keep selected row visible when list exceeds terminal height

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix .gitignore: exclude binaries and generated files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add data-driven tutorial system

Tutorials are stored in workloads.json (not hardcoded), making them
user-editable and extensible.

Data model:
- Add 'tutorial' field (Option<String>) to Workload struct
- Add Tutorial struct and 'tutorials' vec to WorkloadRegistry
- Both use #[serde(default)] for backward compat with existing files

Content (11 tutorials total):
- General: Leader Key, Getting Started
- Per-workload: LSP, DAP, TreeView, Tabs, Telescope, Treesitter,
  OmniSharp (C#), Node (TS/JS), Python

CLI: pnm tutorial [topic]
- No args lists all topics
- Supports workload aliases (e.g. pnm tutorial py)

TUI:
- 't' on instance list opens tutorial browser
- 't' on edit features shows tutorial for highlighted workload
- Scrollable tutorial viewer (j/k, d/u, g/G)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix CI: rename binary to pnm, switch to rustls-tls

- Add [[bin]] name = 'pnm' so the built binary matches packaging
- Switch reqwest from native-tls to rustls-tls to avoid openssl
  dependency issues in cross-compilation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add CI/CD workflows and bump to v1.0.0

- ci.yml: build + test on push/PR to main (Windows, Linux, macOS)
- release.yml: cross-platform release builds on v* tags
  Targets: x86_64-linux, aarch64-linux, x86_64-windows, x86_64-macos, aarch64-macos
- Bump Cargo.toml version to 1.0.0

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: move footer to landing page only

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add footer with copyright notice

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add README and ARCHITECTURE docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: fix duplicate key bug and modularize shared code

- Fix duplicate React key error in HeroAnimation by adding stable
  element ID tracking (ids[]) to SortStep. Merge sort and insertion
  sort create temporary duplicate values in intermediate array states,
  which caused the valueToId map to return the same key for two
  positions. Each sorting algorithm now maintains a parallel ids array
  that tracks element identity through swaps, shifts, and copies.

- Extract shared color palette into app/lib/colors.ts, replacing 5
  independent color constant declarations across components.

- Extract generateArray and barColor into app/lib/sortingUtils.ts,
  removing duplicates from HeroAnimation and SortingVisualizer.

- Remove the fragile valueToId ref pattern from both HeroAnimation
  and SortingVisualizer in favor of step.ids[pos].

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add public-header documentation for include/temporal/ and include/themis/

Add 5 documentation files each for include/temporal/ and include/themis/:

include/temporal/ (v1.6.0, 2026-03-20):
- ARCHITECTURE.md — overview, design principles, interface inventory table
  for all 13 temporal headers, component interaction diagram
- AUDIT.md — 2026-03-22 audit pass; 2 minor findings (CDC overflow semantics,
  RetentionRule equality operators)
- CHANGELOG.md — Keep-a-Changelog format from v1.0.0 to v1.6.0
- ROADMAP.md — completed items, planned features (v1.6.1–v1.9.0),
  6-phase implementation plan, production readiness checklist
- SECURITY.md — threat model table (9 threats), security controls,
  known limitations

include/themis/ (v1.7.0, 2026-03-12):
- ARCHITECTURE.md — overview, design principles, interface inventory table
  for 8 top-level headers + 3 subdirectories, dependency graph
- AUDIT.md — 2026-03-22 audit pass; 1 minor finding (GateResult denial reason)
- CHANGELOG.md — Keep-a-Changelog format from v1.0.0 to v1.7.0
- ROADMAP.md — completed items, planned features (v1.7.1–v2.0.0),
  6-phase implementation plan, production readiness checklist
- SECURITY.md — threat model table (9 threats), fail-closed controls,
  known limitations

All files start with validated status header and cross-reference
../../src/<module>/ for implementation details.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add module documentation for ethics_ai, onnx_clip, rpc_grpc, user_storage_encrypted, and process

Add all missing documentation files (README, ARCHITECTURE, AUDIT, CHANGELOG,
ROADMAP, SECURITY, FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS) for 4 plugin modules, plus AUDIT.md
and SECURITY.md for the existing process module.

Modules documented:
- src/ethics_ai/ (7 files): multi-philosophy ethical discourse engine with RAG
- src/onnx_clip/ (7 files): CLIP ViT-B/32 and ViT-L/14 ONNX image embeddings
- src/rpc_grpc/ (7 files): gRPC server with mTLS and bidirectional streaming
- src/user_storage_encrypted/ (7 files): gocryptfs FUSE + key rotation + multi-tier
- src/process/ (2 files): BPMN/EPK/VCC-VPB Graph-RAG process module

All content is grounded in actual source code. Format follows the established
convention from src/network/ reference files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: rewrite trimDots to avoid repeated string allocations (#8154)

* perf: rewrite trimDots to use index scanning instead of repeated string allocation

The old implementation created a new string on each iteration via substr/slice.
The new implementation scans for the first and last non-dot indices using
charCodeAt, then performs a single substring call. If no trimming is needed,
the original string is returned directly (zero allocations).

Benchmark (20M calls across 10 representative inputs):
- Old: ~628 ms
- New: ~154 ms
- ~4x faster (75% reduction)

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/291bb058-424b-40b5-a64d-5f29c3bdd2bd

* add comment explaining charCode 46 is a dot

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/8f737372-5df9-40fc-9493-8868e1da7a2a

* test: add comprehensive tests for trimDots covering all code paths

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: initialize React + Vite admin panel for chat application

- Set up Vite + React 18 + TypeScript project
- Install Ant Design, React Router 6, Zustand, TanStack Query/Table, Axios, Recharts, React Hook Form + Zod
- Implement feature-based folder structure (features, shared, layouts, routes)
- Add shared types: User, AuthUser, LoginCredentials, DashboardStats, Message, ApiResponse, PaginatedResponse
- Add axios API client with JWT request interceptor and 401 redirect handler
- Add auth, users, and dashboard service layers with mock data fallback
- Implement Zustand auth store with persist middleware
- Build LoginPage with React Hook Form + Zod validation (demo: admin@chat.com / admin123)
- Add ProtectedRoute component for JWT-guarded navigation
- Build AdminLayout with collapsible sidebar, breadcrumb, notification badge, and user dropdown
- Build DashboardPage with 6 stats cards and Recharts line/bar charts
- Build UsersPage with searchable/filterable table, ban/unban with confirmation dialogs
- Configure Ant Design theme with custom primary color
- Add .env.example and .gitignore

commit.message:
perf: optimize revision parsing with charCode-based number parsing (#8151)

* perf: optimize revision parsing with charCode-based number parsing

Replace split('-') with indexOf + substring in parseRevision() to avoid
array allocation. Replace parseInt + substring with manual charCode loop
for multi-digit heights in both parseRevision() and getHeightOfRevision().

Benchmark results (5M iterations):
- parseRevision: 51-102% faster
- getHeightOfRevision multi-digit: 124-160% faster

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/ff63173c-0ac3-4de8-bba5-31a838bed1d1

* test: add unit tests for parseRevision, getHeightOfRevision, createRevision with various height lengths

repository.name: rxdb
repository.description: A fast, local first, reactive Database for JavaScript Applications https://rxdb.info/

commit.message:
perf: optimize now() with integer counter tracking (#8152)

* perf: optimize now() by replacing Math.round with integer counter tracking

Replace floating point arithmetic and Math.round(ret * 100) / 100 rounding
with separate integer tracking of milliseconds and sub-millisecond counter.
This eliminates the need for Math.round() entirely and reduces floating point
operations, achieving ~4.5% faster execution in benchmarks.

Before (1M calls): 56.04ms median
After  (1M calls): 53.64ms median

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/68843f44-e0f2-43d1-a3ca-9a275d38b446

* docs: add clarifying comment for _lastNowSub counter start value

commit.message:
fix: ensure browser test error logs always include a stack trace (#8156)

When certain error types like DOMException are thrown in browser tests,
error.stack can be undefined even though instanceof Error passes.
This changes the handlers to:
- Use duck-typing instead of instanceof to catch cross-realm errors
- Provide a fallback stack trace via new Error().stack when original is missing
- Add stack traces to the non-error branch as well

commit.message:
perf: optimize ensureNotFalsy by separating throw into helper function (#8155)

Move the throw statement into a separate `ensureNotFalsyThrow` helper
function so that V8's TurboFan JIT can more aggressively inline the
main `ensureNotFalsy` function at call sites. The hot path (truthy
values) now has a simpler control flow graph without throw, which
improves inlining heuristics.

Benchmark results (200M iterations, 9 runs, median):
- Before: 628.2 ms
- After:  603.4 ms
- Improvement: ~4% faster


Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/8630a7bf-b42b-48ed-bd18-7b164cb8aebb

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: downgrade Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.* from 4.14.0 to 4.12.0

Studio version, which ships Roslyn 4.12.0. Downgraded all analyzer,
source generator, and related library projects. Benchmark and sandbox
projects that have transitive dependencies requiring >= 4.14.0 (via
BenchmarkDotNet) remain at 4.14.0 since they run standalone.

commit.message:
test(runtime): add comprehensive wire protocol test coverage (#344)

Expand the wire.rs test module from 6 tests to 55, covering all major
behaviours with roundtrip, boundary, and negative/error-path tests.

New coverage areas:
- Varint: zero, 127/128 boundary, u64::MAX, empty buffer, truncated
  multibyte, non-canonical 10th byte
- Zigzag: 0, ±1, i64::MIN/MAX, sign→parity mapping invariant
- Fixed32/64: encode+decode roundtrip, truncated buffer rejection
- HBF header: zero/max payload, null data, too-short buffer, wrong
  magic, wrong version, unknown flags, flag stripping, write_hbf_header
- Decode bytes: truncated payload
- Decode string: roundtrip, empty, truncated, empty buffer
- Decode tag: roundtrip, empty buffer
- Skip field: all four wire types, truncated fixed32/64, unknown type
- buf_has_remaining, buf_reset
- Envelope: empty payload, max msg_type boundary, negative msg_type,
  too-large msg_type, truncated mid-field, unknown wire type

Sabotage-validated three error-path tests to confirm they detect
regressions when the corresponding production checks are removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(analyzers): CVJ-003 now replaces declaration type with bool

The code fix for CVJ-003 replaced .Validate(...) with .EvaluateSchema()
but left explicit variable declaration types (e.g. ValidationContext)
unchanged, producing code that would not compile.

Both fix paths (simple and collector-based) now detect
LocalDeclarationStatementSyntax with an explicit type and replace it
with bool. Added 3 new tests covering explicit type replacement,
collector + type replacement, and var-type preservation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
